rulings.Case Title: JOSH LEWIS et al VS. FUN & GAMES RENTALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT FUN & GAMES RENTALS, STEVEN RESTIVO EVENT SERVICES, LLC, STEVE RESTIVO, THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, UNION STREET ASSOCIATION, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Plaintiff Josh Lewis shall provide responses to Defendants' first sets of form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production, and serve responsive documents, and Plaintiff Anna Koller Lewis shall provide further responses and serve further responsive documents likewise, within 20 days of the date of this order. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted, but only against Plaintiffs' former counsel, Jeanne M. Roberts, Esq., in light of the declarations of Ms. Lewis and her new counsel and the reply filed by Defendants. Within 21 days of the date of this order, Ms. Roberts shall pay monetary sanctions of $1,289.75 to Defendants in care of Defendants' counsel. By 12:00 noon on November 1, 2012, Defendants' counsel shall serve notice of the entry of this order on Ms. Roberts by fax and regular mail in care of the Law Office of Scott L. Woodall, 2340 Santa Rita Road, Suite 5, Pleasanton, CA 94566, which is the address still listed for Ms. Roberts on the online records of the State Bar of California. On its own motion, the Court orders the documents attached to Plaintiff Anna Koller Lewis's responses to requests for production (Exhibit M to the Declaration of Jordan M. Green in support of the motion) to be sealed, as they appear to be private medical records. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Todd Theodora To Appear For Deposition And Produce Docs; Memo Of P&A In Support Of Thereof And Declaration Of Robert A. Schwartz;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC., A CALIFORNIA, MOHAMMAD CHAHINE Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Todd Theodora To Appear For Deposition And Produce Docs; Memo Of P&A In Support Of Thereof And Declaration Of Robert A. Schwartz; Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. The deposition of Todd Theodora is already noticed to reconvene on December 11 and 12, 2012, and there is no good cause shown to compel an earlier deposition. Moving parties' arguments regarding the availability of Mr. Theodora's counsel during trial in the other action are not well taken, as it would be unreasonable and unrealistic to expect counsel to be able to prepare to defend the deposition and then appear at it while in the middle of trial, even on days in which the trial is dark. The motion to compel production of documents at the deposition is denied without prejudice, as the witness's deposition has not yet reconvened and it may turn out that any documents eventually produced will resolve any such issues. The parties' respective requests for monetary sanctions are denied without prejudice to reasserting the same following the reconvened deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANTs TOWERS WATSON AMERICA LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIAbility company, EMB AMERICA LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPany's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action and sustained, without leave to amend, as to sixth and seventh causes of action. For the fifth cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not pled negligent misrepresentation with the requisite specificity against a corporate defendant. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mutual Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) For the fifth and sixth causes of action, Cross-Complainant has not cured the pleading defects from the First Amended Cross-Complaint to support his claims for equitable indemnity. Cross-Complainant has not shown how he can amend the allegations to cure these defects. The court grants the request for judicial notice of Request nos. 1 - 4 as to the filing of these pleadings, but not the truth of the matters asserted. The court denies the request for judicial notice of Request nos. 5 - 7. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 5, defendanTs PETER BRESLER, FAYE LEE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies defendant's motion for summary judgment because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether decedent provided instructions to defendant that only the plaintiff and not any future wife would take under the testamentary instruments. The Court overrules the D'Amico objection. Paragraphs 5-6 of plaintiff's declaration and pages 34:22-35:6 of her deposition are not necessarily contradictory. rulings.Case Title: EDDIE VALADEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The Court denies the motion to quash as to defendants Larry Nichol and Edward Matias (misnamed Larry Nichols and Edward Mathias). The court determines that plaintiff served the person apparently in charge within the meaning of CCP 415.20 because defendants received actual notice of the action and the Court liberally construes the service statutes where the defendants receive actual notice. Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1436-1437. The misspelling of the defendants' names does not render the service fatally defective. Plaintiff is instructed to file an amended complaint with the correct spellings. The Court denies the motion as moot as to defendant Mark Calgaro because evidence indicates that he was personally served on September 28, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE LOPEZ VS. NATALIE WRIGHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ccsf'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 11, Defendant Ccsf'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Inspection Demands And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Plaintiff's faxed discovery responses were not effective service of the same absent a written agreement for service of papers by facsimile transmission. (Code Civ. Proc., § 1013, subd. (e).) There was no such written agreement. Plaintiff's failure to serve the original responses by mail or other means is thus treated as a failure to respond at all to Defendants' interrogatories and inspection demands. Therefore, within 20 days of this order, Plaintiff shall serve full and complete responses, without objections, to each and all of Defendants' form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and inspection demands. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $500.00, assessed against and payable by Plaintiff's counsel to Defendant's counsel within 20 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT M. CAIN MD VS. KYO-YA HOTELS & RESORTS, LP DBA PALACE HOTEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Deft'S Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And An Ime In California
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT KYO-YA COMPANY, LTD, STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC Ntc Of Hearing On Deft'S Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And An Ime In California Pro Tem Judge Jim Weixel, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. The factors set forth in Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.260 do not outweigh section 2025.250's presumption that the deposition of a natural person shall be taken within 75 miles of his residence. Plaintiff did not select the forum, strictly speaking, as he cannot be said to have chosen to sustain an alleged injury while visiting another city. There is no reason to believe Plaintiff will not be available to testify at trial. As pointed out by Plaintiff, it would cause inconvenience to Plaintiff to travel to San Francisco for a deposition. It is not feasible to conduct Plaintiff's deposition by written questions or to obtain the same information through other methods of discovery. Although Defendants point out that other witnesses may have to travel to San Francisco, Defendants do not explain who they are, and if Defendant refer to Plaintiff's treating physicians, those witnesses would have to be deposed in Austin anyway (and could be deposed at the same time as Plaintiff) as the Court cannot compel their appearance in California. Finally, there is no difference between the expense of requiring Plaintiff to travel to San Francisco and that of requiring Defendants' counsel to travel to Austin. Furthermore, as pointed out by Plaintiff, there is no statutory provision for requiring a plaintiff in a personal injury action to submit to a physical examination more than 75 miles from his or her residence. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 2032.220, subd. (a)(2).) The only statutory provision allowing examinations of any kind to be conducted more than 75 miles from the residence of the examinee pertains to examinations other than those of a plaintiff in a personal injury action. (§ 2032.310, subd. (a); § 2032.320, subd. (e).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to appeals@jimweixel.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Counsel for Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order accordingly repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: MARK MIGDAL et al VS. MASHA LEVINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Immediate Sale Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 31, 2012, Line 16, Defendants Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's Motion For Immediate Sale Of Real Property. Granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 872.120 and 872.130 and the Court's broad equitable power in partition actions. The property located at 461 and 465 Bellevue Avenue, Oakland, California, shall be sold. The proceeds from the sale shall be placed in a blocked account pending resolution of this case. It is not necessary for the Court to determine the property interests of the parties and rights of any lien holders at this time. In any event, it does not appear that the parties' respective interests in the property are disputed.
